Data Management
Data Summary
⚡ Supabase Backend
✅ Connected to Supabase
⚠️ Running offline (localStorage only) — fill in credentials below to go live
Setup SQL — run this once in Supabase SQL Editor
create table if not exists tle_leads    (id bigint primary key, data jsonb);
create table if not exists tle_tasks    (id bigint primary key, data jsonb);
create table if not exists tle_calls    (id bigint primary key, data jsonb);
create table if not exists tle_notes    (id bigint primary key, data jsonb);
create table if not exists tle_settings (id bigint primary key, data jsonb);

-- Allow public read/write (adjust with RLS when adding auth)
alter table tle_leads    enable row level security;
alter table tle_tasks    enable row level security;
alter table tle_calls    enable row level security;
alter table tle_notes    enable row level security;
alter table tle_settings enable row level security;

create policy "allow all" on tle_leads    for all using (true) with check (true);
create policy "allow all" on tle_tasks    for all using (true) with check (true);
create policy "allow all" on tle_calls    for all using (true) with check (true);
create policy "allow all" on tle_notes    for all using (true) with check (true);
create policy "allow all" on tle_settings for all using (true) with check (true);
create table if not exists tle_msg_events   (id bigint primary key, data jsonb);
create table if not exists tle_msg_templates(id bigint primary key, data jsonb);
create table if not exists tle_dial_lists   (id bigint primary key, data jsonb);
alter table tle_msg_events    enable row level security;
alter table tle_msg_templates enable row level security;
alter table tle_dial_lists    enable row level security;
create policy "allow all" on tle_msg_events    for all using (true) with check (true);
create policy "allow all" on tle_msg_templates for all using (true) with check (true);
create policy "allow all" on tle_dial_lists    for all using (true) with check (true);
How it works: Every time you save a lead, log a call, or update a task — the change writes to localStorage instantly (so the UI stays fast) and also syncs to Supabase in the background. Your VA opens the same HTML file, enters the same credentials, and sees the same data. Use ⬇ Pull to fetch latest data from Supabase on any device.
VA Access Guide

How your VA should use this CRM safely:

📂 Opening the file
Send your VA the HTML file once. They open it in Chrome or Edge, go to Settings → Supabase Backend, paste the Project URL and Anon Key you share with them, and click Save & Connect. From that point they're working on the same live database as you.
👤 Adding leads
Use the ＋ Add Lead button. Or import via CSV. Duplicate detection runs automatically on phone and email.
📋 Updating stages
Click any lead to open the detail panel → Edit Lead → update the Stage field. Or drag cards on the Pipeline Board.
📞 Logging calls
Click the 📞 button on any lead row or inside the detail panel. Select the outcome, add notes, set next follow-up date.
🔐 How your VA logs in
Once Supabase is connected, your VA's "login" is simply the same Project URL and Anon Key you use. There's no separate username or password system needed at this stage.

Step-by-step for your VA:
1. Download the CRM HTML file (you send it once — it never changes)
2. Open it in Chrome or Edge
3. Go to Settings → Supabase Backend
4. Paste the Project URL and Anon Key you share with them
5. Click Save & Connect — the app reloads and pulls live data

From that point: every lead they add, every call they log, every stage they update — appears on your screen instantly the next time you open the CRM (or hit Pull).

VA permissions: Switch their Role to VA in the User Profile section above. This hides revenue figures, the Reports tab, and Settings — they only see what they need to work.
Read top to bottom first

VA Guide

Everything you need to operate this CRM correctly. Read this once before touching any leads. Then keep it open as a reference while you work.

—
Total Leads
1
First-Time Setup
What to do before starting any work
1
Open in Chrome
Always use Google Chrome. The CRM is not tested in Safari or Firefox and some features may not work correctly.
2
Check Supabase is connected
Go to Settings → confirm the Supabase URL and Key are filled in. The sync indicator in the top bar should show green. If it shows red, contact Calum before making any changes.
3
Set your role to VA
Go to Settings → Role → select VA (Limited Access). This hides revenue figures that are for Calum's eyes only.
4
Check the sidebar is loading properly
Click each main section — Leads, Outreach, Tasks — to confirm data is showing. If something looks empty, use the Pull from Supabase button in Settings.
🚫
Never touch "Clear All Data"
This button in Settings permanently deletes all leads and records. It cannot be undone. Do not click it under any circumstances.
2
Daily Opening Routine
60-second start of day process — do this every session
🌐
Step 1
Open the CRM in Chrome and wait for it to fully load
👤
Step 2
Click Leads — confirm the lead list is populated and not blank
✓
Step 3
Click Tasks — check what tasks are due today and prioritise those first
🔥
Step 4
Filter leads by Replied → Warm → Contacted. Work those first before anything else
☁️
Step 5
Confirm the sync dot is green before making any updates. If orange, click Pull from Supabase in Settings
3
Adding a New Lead
How to enter leads properly every time
Required Fields
RequiredFull Name — First name then last name, capitalised. e.g. James McCormack
RequiredPipeline — Almost always Outbound for new LinkedIn leads
RequiredStage — New leads always start at Untouched unless told otherwise
If knownEmail — Copy exactly as given, lowercase
If knownLinkedIn URL — Full URL from the browser bar. e.g. linkedin.com/in/jamesmccormack
If knownRole / Seniority — Select the closest match from the dropdown
How to Grade a Lead
GRADE 1
Highest Priority
Partner, Director, Senior Barrister. Strong ICA fit. Likely to convert.
GRADE 2
Good Fit
Solicitor, Associate, Legal Counsel. Good potential but less certain.
GRADE 3
Lower Priority
Junior, Trainee, or unclear role. Work these after Grades 1 and 2.
4
Stage Rules
What every stage means and exactly when to move someone
Untouched Lead is in the system but has not been contacted yet. Default starting stage.
Attempted You tried to call or reach out but got no response. Move here after a dial with no answer.
Contacted You spoke to them briefly or sent a message that was delivered. First real contact made.
⭐ Replied They responded to your message. This is a hot lead. Move here immediately when they reply and tell Calum.
Qualified Lead has confirmed interest and fits the ideal client profile. Usually set by Calum.
Call Booked A call is scheduled. Date and time should be in the notes. Calum handles from here.
No-Show They booked a call but didn't show up. Log the outcome and add a follow-up task.
Nurture Good lead but not ready right now. Keep warm, follow up in 4–6 weeks. Set a task.
✅ Won Client signed up. Only Calum sets this stage. Do not use it yourself.
❌ Lost Lead said no or is no longer interested. Add a note with the reason before moving them here.
5
Logging a Call
How to record call outcomes correctly every time
The 6 Call Outcome Buttons
📵
No Answer
Phone rang but nobody picked up. Stage moves to Attempted Contact.
📨
Voicemail
Left a voicemail. Add a note with what you said. Set a follow-up task.
🚫
Bad Number
Number is wrong or disconnected. Add a note. Do not attempt again without a new number.
✅
Spoke — Good
Called and had a good conversation. Always add a note with what was discussed. Move stage forward.
📅
Call Booked
They agreed to a call. Add the date and time in the notes field immediately. Move stage to Call Booked.
🔁
Call Back Later
They asked you to call back. Set a task with the specific date and time they gave you.
Steps to Log a Call
1
Find the lead in the Leads list and click their name to open the detail panel
2
Click the Log Call button in the detail panel
3
Select the correct outcome from the 6 buttons above
4
Write a short note in the notes field — what they said, any objections, what happens next
5
Update their stage if it has changed, then click Save
❌ Never
• Log a call without selecting an outcome
• Leave the notes field blank after a good conversation
• Skip updating the stage if it has changed
• Log calls for other team members — only log your own
✅ Always
• Log every call, even no-answers
• Add a note with what was said on every connected call
• Set a follow-up task if the next action is not immediate
• Notify Calum immediately when someone replies or books
6
Updating a Lead
What to do when something changes
💬 When they reply on LinkedIn
Open the lead → change stage to Replied → add a note with exactly what they said → notify Calum straight away. Do not reply on behalf of Calum without specific instructions.
🔄 When their stage changes
Open the lead → click Edit → update the Stage dropdown to reflect what happened → always add a short note explaining why the stage changed → Save.
📝 When to add a note
Add a note any time: they say something important, you learn new information about them, a call happens, they give an objection, or any update that Calum would want to know about.
📌 When to add a task
Set a task whenever: you promised to follow up, they asked to be called at a specific time, a call needs to be rescheduled, or the next action is more than 24 hours away. Always set a due date.
7
Running a Dial Block
The order of priority when calling leads
Priority Order — Always work in this order
1
Replied
These are hot leads. They responded to outreach. Call these first, every single session.
2
Warm Leads
Temperature set to Hot or Warm. These are in active conversations and should be called second.
3
Contacted
Had some contact but no strong signal yet. Work through these after Replied and Warm.
Steps
1
Go to Dial Blocks in the sidebar and select or create a dial block session
2
Filter by stage: start with Replied, then Warm, then Contacted
3
Call each lead, log the outcome immediately after hanging up — do not batch-log calls
4
Update stage and add a note for every connected call before moving to the next lead
5
When finished, confirm all outcomes are logged and push sync to Supabase
💡
Use Smart Views to speed this up
Go to Smart Views in the sidebar — pre-built filtered views let you instantly see Replied leads, Hot leads, and leads with phone numbers. Much faster than manually filtering every session.
8
End of Day Checklist
What must be done before finishing each session
Quick Reference Card
Screenshot this and keep it open while working
New lead to add
Leads → ＋ Add Lead → fill Name, Pipeline (Outbound), Stage (Untouched), Grade → Save
Lead replied on LinkedIn
Find lead → open detail → Edit → Stage = Replied → add note with what they said → Save → notify Calum
Call answered — good conversation
Open lead → Log Call → Spoke Good → write note with what was said → update stage → Save
No answer on call
Open lead → Log Call → No Answer → stage moves to Attempted Contact automatically → set follow-up task
Lead needs follow-up
Open lead → Tasks tab → ＋ Add Task → set description and due date → Save. Stage stays the same.
Lead not interested
Open lead → Edit → Stage = Lost → add note with their reason → Save. Do not delete the lead.
Lead booked a call
Open lead → Edit → Stage = Call Booked → add note with date and time booked → Save → notify Calum immediately
☁️ End of day sync
Settings → scroll to Supabase section → click Push to Supabase → wait for ✅ Pushed confirmation
